 {"id":"bd-10vp","title":"Feature: Extension Event Hook Dispatch","description":"# Feature: Extension Event Hook Dispatch

## Overview

This feature enables the agent to call INTO extensions at lifecycle events. Extensions register handlers via pi.on(eventName, handler), and the agent dispatches events at appropriate points.

## Distinction from Events Hostcall

- **Events Hostcall (pi.events)**: Extension-initiated, asks runtime about events
- **Event Hook Dispatch**: Agent-initiated, calls extension handlers at lifecycle points

## Event Types (from EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE.md §12.7)

### Agent Lifecycle Events

| Event | When | Handler Can |
|-------|------|-------------|
| startup | Agent initialized | Access session, configure |
| agent_start | Before first API call | Modify system prompt |
| agent_end | After agent loop ends | Log, cleanup |

### Turn Lifecycle Events

| Event | When | Handler Can |
|-------|------|-------------|
| turn_start | Before provider.stream() | Modify context |
| turn_end | After response processed | Log, analyze |

### Tool Events

| Event | When | Handler Can |
|-------|------|-------------|
| tool_call | Before tool execution | Block, modify |
| tool_result | After tool execution | Modify result |

### Session Events

| Event | When | Handler Can |
|-------|------|-------------|
| session_before_switch | Before session switch | Cancel switch |
| session_before_fork | Before session fork | Cancel fork |

### Input Events

| Event | When | Handler Can |
|-------|------|-------------|
| input | Before processing user message | Transform, block |

## Event Handler Return Values

Different events allow different return values:

```typescript
// tool_call: Can block execution
interface ToolCallEventResult {
    block?: boolean;
    reason?: string;
}

// tool_result: Can modify the result
interface ToolResultEventResult {
    content?: ContentBlock[];
    details?: unknown;
}

// session events: Return false to cancel
type SessionEventResult = boolean;
```

## Implementation Architecture

```
Agent Loop (src/agent.rs)
    │
    ├─── dispatch_event("turn_start", context)
    │           │
    │           ▼
    │    PiJsRuntime.__pi_dispatch_extension_event()
    │           │
    │           ▼
    │    [Extension handlers called in sequence]
    │           │
    │           ▼
    │    Results aggregated and returned
    │
    └─── [Continue with turn]
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All documented events dispatch at correct points
- [ ] Blocking events (tool_call) can prevent execution
- [ ] Modifying events (tool_result) can change data
- [ ] Cancellable events (session_*) return boolean
- [ ] Handler errors don't crash agent
- [ ] Extension context passed correctly to handlers","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:55:58.456544979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:09:31.483635020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:09:31.483570760Z","close_reason":"All 7 sub-tasks complete: EventDispatcher created (bd-tg4w), agent lifecycle events (bd-13gm), turn lifecycle events (bd-5yyc), tool_call dispatch (bd-35hz), tool_result dispatch (bd-318h), input event (bd-s1hx), session events (bd-3avm).  {"id":"bd-11cz","title":"PiWasm: Spec WebAssembly JS subset + glue patterns","description":"# Goal
Lock the *minimum sufficient* WebAssembly JS API surface for PiWasm (WebAssembly-in-JS) to support the pinned extension corpus without Node/Bun.

# Why
- QuickJS does not ship WebAssembly; the bridge must be explicit, deterministic, capability-gated, and testable.
- A crisp spec prevents accidental Node/Bun scope creep.

# Deliverables
- Supported API list (MVP): WebAssembly.instantiate(bytes, imports), WebAssembly.compile(bytes) (optional), WebAssembly.Module/Instance (if needed), WebAssembly.Memory (required).
- Explicitly unsupported APIs (with required error shapes + diagnostics).
- Glue compatibility matrix: common JS+WASM bundle patterns (Emscripten-style) we intend to support and their required semantics.
- Determinism + security invariants: budgets, import gating, no ambient OS.

# Acceptance
- Spec is detailed enough to implement without consulting external notes.
- Includes example call shapes + expected failure mapping.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:24.163710747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:50:17.799626703Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:50:17.799535242Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11cz","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2630,"issue_id":"bd-11cz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## PiWasm JS API Spec (Final)

### Supported APIs (MVP)

#### WebAssembly.compile(bufferSource) → Promise<Module>
- Accepts: Uint8Array, ArrayBuffer, or array of bytes
- Returns: A compiled Module handle
- Errors: WebAssembly.CompileError on invalid bytes

#### WebAssembly.instantiate(bufferSource, importObject?) → Promise<{module, instance}>
- Accepts: WASM bytes + optional import object
- Import object: { module_name: { func_name: func, ... }, ... }
- Returns: { module: Module, instance: Instance }
- Overload: instantiate(Module, importObject?) → Promise<Instance>
- Errors: WebAssembly.LinkError on import mismatch

#### WebAssembly.Instance
- instance.exports → object with exported funcs + memory

#### WebAssembly.Memory
- new Memory({initial, maximum?}) → standalone memory
- memory.buffer → ArrayBuffer (snapshot of linear memory)
- memory.grow(delta) → previous page count (or -1 on failure)

#### WebAssembly.RuntimeError / CompileError / LinkError
- Standard error constructors for WASM trap/compile/link failures

### Explicitly Unsupported (MVP)
- instantiateStreaming/compileStreaming → NotSupportedError
- Table, Global, validate → not yet supported error
- Shared memory / atomics → not supported

### Memory Sync Protocol
1. After instantiate: copy wasmtime memory → JS ArrayBuffer
2. Before export call: copy JS ArrayBuffer → wasmtime memory
3. After export call: copy wasmtime memory → JS ArrayBuffer
4. On grow: create new larger ArrayBuffer, old reference stale

### Import Handling (MVP - core polyfill only)
- Module imports satisfied by generic Rust stubs (return 0/no-op)
- Full JS callback imports deferred to bd-ltk7
- WASI/Emscripten-specific stubs in bd-ltk7

### Security Invariants
- Memory limited to max_memory_pages from policy
- Structured tracing logs for compile/instantiate
- No raw OS access from WASM execution
- All operations capability-gated","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:50:03Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-11k","title":"Implement provider streaming tests using VCR cassettes","description":"# Implement provider streaming tests using VCR cassettes

## Goal
Write comprehensive provider streaming tests using recorded VCR cassettes (no live network).

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests.
- Prefer `#[asupersync::test]` or an explicit `Runtime::new().block_on(...)` wrapper to match the active runtime.
- Force `VCR_MODE=playback` + `VCR_CASSETTE_DIR` for all tests.
- Set `PI_CONFIG_PATH` and `PI_SESSIONS_DIR` to temp dirs; assert zero live HTTP calls.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) to log cassette name/path, request summary, event timeline, and diffs.
- On failure, dump expected vs actual event sequences + stop reasons.

## Test File Structure
```rust
// tests/provider_streaming.rs

mod vcr;
use vcr::VcrRecorder;

mod anthropic {
    use super::*;

    #[asupersync::test]
    async fn test_simple_text_response() {
        let vcr = VcrRecorder::new("anthropic_simple_text");
        let provider = AnthropicProvider::new_with_client(vcr.client());

        let stream = provider.stream(&context, &options).await.unwrap();
        let events: Vec<_> = stream.collect().await;

        assert!(matches!(events[0], StreamEvent::Start { .. }));
        assert!(matches!(events[1], StreamEvent::TextStart { .. }));
        // ... verify all events
        let final_msg = events.last().unwrap();
        assert_eq!(final_msg.stop_reason, StopReason::Stop);
    }
}
```

## Test Categories

### Streaming Event Sequence Tests
Verify correct event ordering:
- Start → TextStart → TextDelta* → TextEnd → Done
- Start → ThinkingStart → ThinkingDelta* → ThinkingEnd → TextStart → ... → Done
- Start → ToolCallStart → ToolCallDelta* → ToolCallEnd → Done(ToolUse)

### Content Parsing Tests
Verify content is parsed correctly:
- Text content extraction
- Thinking content extraction
- Tool call argument parsing (JSON)
- Multiple content blocks

### Usage Tracking Tests
Verify token counting:
- Input tokens
- Output tokens
- Cache read/write tokens
- Cost calculation

### Error Handling Tests
Verify errors are surfaced correctly:
- Rate limit with retry-after
- Auth failure message
- Bad request details
- Server error handling

## Providers to Test
1. Anthropic (primary focus, most complex)
2. OpenAI (after Anthropic pattern established)
3. Gemini (different response format)
4. Azure (similar to OpenAI)

## Dependencies
- bd-1pf (VCR infrastructure)
- bd-30u (Anthropic cassettes recorded)

## Files
- tests/provider_streaming.rs
- tests/provider_streaming/anthropic.rs
- tests/provider_streaming/openai.rs
- tests/provider_streaming/gemini.rs
- tests/provider_streaming/azure.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 20+ Anthropic streaming tests
- [ ] 10+ OpenAI streaming tests
- [ ] 10+ Gemini streaming tests
- [ ] 5+ Azure streaming tests
- [ ] All tests use VCR (no live API)
- [ ] Event sequences validated
- [ ] Usage tracking validated
- [ ] Error handling validated
- [ ] Logs include cassette path + event timeline

 {"id":"bd-193","title":"E2E: Security regression suite (capability & sandbox)","description":"# Goal
Build **end-to-end security tests** that prove capability enforcement and sandbox boundaries with detailed logs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Test cases:
  - path traversal + symlink escape
  - forbidden host/network policy
  - env key denial
  - oversized payload / output limits
  - denied `exec` capability blocks `child_process.spawn` and `process.kill`
  - wasm bridge limits: memory/table limits, denied imports, trap mapping (PiWasm)
- Each failure must emit a structured log entry with capability, scope, and deny reason.
- Negative tests ensure no partial side effects occurred.

# Why (User Value)
- Trustworthy, repeatable proof that PiJS is safer than Node/Bun.

# Tests
- E2E scripts that run with verbose logging, produce a summary report, and archive per-test artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

Purpose
- Provide a deterministic “source checklist” so future agents can repeat discovery and converge on the same candidate set.

A) Official Pi sources (baseline)
- pi-mono examples/extensions README + dirs (already vendored once; keep as must-pass baseline)
- buildwithpi.ai packages + docs (if package JSON is exposed, treat it as authoritative metadata)

B) OpenClaw / marketplace ecosystems (new major surface)

- Prefer machine-readable indexes (JSON feeds, GraphQL endpoints, API responses) over scraping HTML.
- Export raw dumps (with timestamps) so the inventory can be regenerated.

C) GitHub repo discovery (keyword-based)
Run repo searches, record top N results + reasons, and archive query strings.
- Query ideas (adjust to reduce noise):
  - "pi agent" extension
  - "buildwithpi" extension
  - "pi-mono" extension
  - "pi" "registerTool" extension
  - topic-based: topic:pi-extension OR topic:pi-agent OR topic:buildwithpi (if topics exist)

D) GitHub code discovery (signature-based)
Goal: find repos that contain *actual extension entrypoints*.
- Search for common registration patterns:
  - "registerTool(" AND ("export default" OR "ExtensionAPI" OR "ctx.")
  - "registerCommand(" OR "/"-command patterns
  - "registerProvider(" (custom providers)
  - "onEvent" / lifecycle hook names (session/tool/cancel)
  - "ui." / rpc UI calls used by extensions
- Heuristic: prefer hits in TypeScript/JavaScript; then validate as “true Pi extension” by checking for Pi protocol usage.

E) npm discovery
- Search npm for packages mentioning Pi Agent / buildwithpi / pi-mono / extension API.
- Capture download counts + dependents (popularity evidence).

F) Cross-reference mining (mentions)
- Search README/docs/issues across discovered repos for:

  - Links to gists or extension bundles
- This tends to find “hidden” but widely used extensions.

Required output

- For each query: the date/time, tool used (GitHub UI/API/gh), and how many candidates it yielded.
- A “noise notes” section: which queries were too broad and what filters improved them.

 {"id":"bd-1mh","title":"Update session picker to use SQLite index","description":"# Update session picker to use SQLite index

## Goal
Replace filesystem walking in session picker with fast SQLite index queries.

## Current Implementation (src/session_picker.rs)
Currently scans filesystem:
```rust
// Slow: walks all session directories
let sessions = walkdir::WalkDir::new(&sessions_dir)
    .into_iter()
    .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
    .filter(|e| e.path().extension() == Some("jsonl"))
    .collect();
```

## New Implementation
```rust
use crate::session_index::SessionIndex;

impl SessionPicker {
    pub fn load_sessions(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
        let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
        index.list_sessions(cwd)
    }
}
```

## UI Changes
- Display indexed metadata directly (no file reads needed)
- Show message count from index
- Show session name from index
- Faster render (no filesystem I/O)

## Fallback Behavior
If index unavailable or empty:
```rust
fn load_sessions_with_fallback(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
    // Try index first
    if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
        if let Ok(sessions) = index.list_sessions(cwd) {
            if !sessions.is_empty() {
                return Ok(sessions);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem (and populate index)
    let sessions = load_sessions_from_filesystem(cwd)?;
    // Optionally trigger reindex in background
    Ok(sessions)
}
```

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: Session picker shows indexed sessions
- Test: Picker falls back to filesystem if needed
- Test: Performance improved (measure time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Picker queries index by default
- [ ] Fallback to filesystem works
- [ ] UI displays index metadata
- [ ] Noticeably faster for 50+ sessions","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1nlkn","title":"FUZZ-P1.1: SSE Parser — Expand proptest coverage for UTF-8, BOM, oversized fields, bare CR","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:54:14.919432520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:59:00.109427965Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T19:59:00.109401466Z","close_reason":"Completed: expanded SSE proptests + RCH validations","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","proptest","sse"],"comments":[{"id":3644,"issue_id":"bd-1nlkn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## FUZZ-P1.1: SSE Parser Proptest Expansion

### Background
The SSE parser (src/sse.rs, 902 lines) already has ONE proptest: `sse_chunking_invariant` (line 883) with 64 cases that validates parsing is chunk-boundary-independent. This is good but only scratches the surface.

### Current Code Under Test
- `process_line()` (line 52-86): Parses individual SSE field lines via `split_once(':')`
- `feed_into()` (line 89-162): Processes stream data, handles CRLF/LF/CR line endings
- `flush()` (line 179-201): Handles incomplete streams
- `SseStream` UTF-8 handling (lines 238-300): Handles incomplete multibyte sequences across chunks

### Specific Risks to Fuzz
1. **UTF-8 edge cases**: Incomplete multibyte sequences split across chunks (lines 254-271). The code accumulates bytes in a leftover buffer and tries to decode — fuzz the boundary between valid/invalid UTF-8.
2. **BOM handling**: BOM stripping at position 0 is explicit (lines 98-102). Test BOM at non-zero positions, multiple BOMs, BOM split across chunks.
3. **Bare CR handling**: Lines 126-132 handle bare CR (\r without \n). The parser must not confuse bare CR with CRLF.
4. **Oversized data fields**: Lines 62-63 append to `current_data` without bounds. Generate events with multi-megabyte data: fields to test memory behavior.
5. **Null bytes in id field**: Already rejected at line 67 — verify this with proptest.
6. **Empty field names**: What happens with `:\n` (colon at start of line)?
7. **Retry field**: The `retry:` field should parse as integer (line 73-78). Fuzz with non-numeric, negative, overflow values.
8. **Duplicate fields**: Multiple `data:` or `event:` fields in one event.

### Implementation Approach
Add new proptest strategies to the existing test module at line 381+:
- `utf8_edge_strategy()`: Generates strings with multibyte chars at chunk boundaries
- `bom_strategy()`: Tests BOM placement variations
- `line_ending_strategy()`: All combinations of \r, \n, \r\n, missing terminators
- `oversized_data_strategy()`: Data fields from 1KB to 10MB
- `retry_field_strategy()`: Non-numeric, negative, overflow retry values

### Invariants to Assert
- Parsing a complete stream in one chunk vs. arbitrary chunk boundaries MUST produce identical events (existing test)
- No panic on any valid or invalid SSE input
- BOM at position 0 is stripped; BOM elsewhere is preserved
- Null bytes in id: field are rejected (event has no id)
- UTF-8 replacement characters appear for invalid byte sequences, never panic

### Files to Modify
- src/sse.rs (add proptest strategies and new test functions to existing test module)

### Acceptance Criteria
- At least 5 new proptest functions targeting the risks above
- Minimum 256 cases per property (up from 64)
- All tests pass with `cargo test sse::tests` on nightly
- Any bugs found are fixed inline","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:54:54Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1o6l","title":"Extension load-time benchmarks (all 60 official, TS vs Rust)","description":"# Extension load-time benchmarks (all 60 official, TS vs Rust)

- P99 < 100ms for Rust
- Rust <= 2x TypeScript for any extension
- Rust median <= TypeScript median

 {"id":"bd-2ok9","title":"E2E: Message injection + session control with verbose logging","description":"End-to-end test for message injection and session control APIs.

## Scenarios

### 1. Message Injection Flow
- Extension sends pi.sendMessage({customType: 'bookmark'})
- Verify message appears in session JSONL
- Verify display=true shows in TUI output

### 2. User Message Injection
- Extension sends pi.sendUserMessage('test prompt')
- Verify agent turn triggered
- Verify steering modes work (steer interrupts, followUp queues)

### 3. Session Metadata Control
- Set session name via pi.setSessionName()
- Verify name persists in session file
- Set entry label via pi.setLabel()

### 4. Tool Management
- Disable a tool via pi.setActiveTools()
- Verify tool no longer available
- Re-enable and verify restored

### 5. Model Control
- Change model via pi.setModel()
- Verify provider switch
- Adjust thinking level and verify

## JSONL Logging Format

 {"id":"bd-3odv","title":"Epic: Differential Extension Conformance Testing (TS-Rust Oracle)","description":"# Epic: Differential Extension Conformance Testing (TS-Rust Oracle)

## The Core Principle

Every extension that works in pi-mono TypeScript runtime MUST produce IDENTICAL behavior in our Rust QuickJS runtime, with ZERO source code modifications. This is not a theoretical goal -- it is a hard requirement for production readiness.

## Why Differential Testing (Not Fixture Replay)

Previous approach: capture TS outputs once, save as fixtures, replay in Rust.
Problem: fixtures are stale, hard to maintain, don't catch edge cases.

New approach: Run the SAME unmodified extension code through BOTH runtimes simultaneously, with the SAME inputs/mocks, and compare outputs directly. The TypeScript pi-mono runtime IS the oracle. This is differential/oracle testing.

Benefits:
- No fixture staleness -- always testing against live TS behavior
- Catches subtle differences in event ordering, error messages, type coercion
- Can test with ANY extension, not just ones we have written fixtures for
- Easy to add new extensions to the corpus -- just drop them in

## Architecture

Extension Source (.ts file) -> BOTH pi-mono (TS via Bun/jiti) AND Rust (swc+QuickJS) -> Compare outputs (registrations, event responses, hostcall results) -> PASS or FAIL

## Corpus (collected)

1,167+ TypeScript files across 5 tiers:
- 60 official pi-mono example extensions
- 58 community extensions
- 63 npm registry extensions
- 23 third-party GitHub extensions
- 7 agent collection extensions

## Key Technical Details

- pi-mono location: legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/
- Extension loader (TS): src/core/extensions/loader.ts -- uses jiti for TS loading
- Extension types (TS): src/core/extensions/types.ts -- ExtensionAPI, Extension, events
- Extension runner (TS): src/core/extensions/runner.ts -- lifecycle management
- Rust runtime: src/extensions.rs + src/extensions_js.rs -- QuickJS-based
- Bun 1.3.8: Available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun
- Artifacts: tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/

## Success Criteria

- 100% of official pi-mono extensions (60) produce identical output in both runtimes
- 90%+ of community extensions produce identical output
- Performance within 2x of TypeScript for load time, 1.5x for event dispatch
- Zero crashes, zero panics, zero undefined behavior
- CI pipeline runs full suite on every PR
- Machine-readable conformance report generated per run","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:13:53.326652907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:55:33.743363158Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:55:23.921140367Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3485,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SUPERSEDES EXISTING BEADS: This epic replaces the previous fragmented conformance testing beads (bd-6vcm, bd-2jlj, bd-c6xq, bd-2bis) with a unified DIFFERENTIAL TESTING approach. Key change: instead of capturing TS fixtures once and replaying in Rust, we run BOTH runtimes on every test and compare directly. The TS pi-mono runtime IS the oracle.

RELATED EXISTING BEADS: bd-6vcm (Feature: Extension Conformance Testing), bd-2jlj (Epic: Full Pi Extension Conformance), bd-c6xq (Reference capture harness), bd-2bis (Fixture-based conformance tests), bd-382l (Phase 1: Research & Source Acquisition), bd-2vrw (Phase 2: Test Infrastructure). These should be considered superseded by this epic's phases.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:41Z"},{"id":3486,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORPUS INVENTORY: As of 2026-02-05, we have 1,167 TypeScript files across 5 tiers in tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/:

- 58 community extensions: mitsuhiko(10), hjanuschka(14), tmustier(12), nicobailon(6), qualisero(7), prateekmedia(6), others(3)

- 23 third-party GitHub repos: aliou-pi-extensions(59 sub-exts), w-winter-dot314(40 sub-exts), etc.
- 7 agent collection directories

Multi-extension collections (aliou=59, w-winter=40) inflate individual entry count to 280+ extensions. After validation, expect 200-300 genuine loadable extensions.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:43Z"},{"id":3487,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE NOTES:

TS RUNTIME (pi-mono):
- loader.ts uses jiti (JIT TS loader by unjs)
- Extension factory: export default function(pi: ExtensionAPI) { ... }
- Registration methods write to Extension object: handlers, tools, commands, flags, shortcuts, messageRenderers
- Action methods delegate to shared ExtensionRuntime: sendMessage, exec, setModel, etc.
- Action methods throw during load (by design -- only registration happens at load time)
- Bun 1.3.8 available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun
- pi-mono source at legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/

RUST RUNTIME:
- extensions_js.rs: JsExtensionLoadSpec, swc TS compilation, QuickJS execution
- extensions.rs: CompatibilityScanner (line 127-414), ExtensionManager, dispatch
- JsExtensionSnapshot captures: tools, slash_commands, shortcuts, flags, event_hooks, providers
- Hostcall bridge: JS pi.events(op,payload) -> Rust dispatch_hostcall_events()
- Session bridge: JS pi.session(op,payload) -> Rust dispatch_hostcall_session()
- ExtensionSession trait: get_state, get_messages, set_name, set_model, set_label

SHARED CONCERNS:

 {"id":"bd-3ok7w","title":"[AUTH-5] /login provider listing: show available OAuth providers and status","description":"## Problem

/login with no provider argument defaults to the current provider. Users cannot discover which providers support OAuth.

## Solution

### /login with No Args Shows Provider Table
```
Available OAuth providers:
  anthropic  ✅ Authenticated (expires in 23 hours)
  openai     ❌ Not authenticated
  google     ❌ Not authenticated

Extension providers:
  my-custom  ✅ Authenticated

Usage: /login <provider>
```

### Implementation
- Enumerate built-in OAuth providers (anthropic, openai, google)
- Check auth.json for credentials for each
- For OAuth credentials, show expiry status
- Enumerate extension-registered providers with OAuth configs
- Show table using tui.rs Table rendering

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: /login handler (no-arg case)
- src/auth.rs: Add method to list credential status per provider

- [ ] Shows authentication status for each provider
- [ ] Shows expiry time for OAuth tokens
